theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Energy (+3.42%) and Consumer Staples (+0.83%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.69%) and Consumer Discretionary (-1.32%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -1.24% versus +0.58% for defensives and +0.46% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained -0.36% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -1.53%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at -0.81%. The VIX rose 12.43% to 16.91, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.68% to $3,999.00, crude oil was little changed at $77.84, and bitcoin slipped 3.07% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
